prompt.prompt_text:
You are analyzing a television commercial for corpus-linguistic and multimodal discourse analysis.

You are given sampled frames from a video clip. The frames are in chronological order.
Treat the frames as a temporal sequence from one commercial, not as independent images.

Describe the visual content of the commercial as it unfolds over time.
Describe only what is visually supported by the frames.

Do not guess dialogue, slogans, music, voice-over, or other audio content.
If something is unclear or not visible in the sampled frames, say so.

output.response_text:
The commercial begins with a soft, slightly blurred coastal or tropical scene. Palm fronds and possibly flowers appear in the foreground on the left, with bright ocean waves or surf visible in the background. The image has a warm, sunlit look and is framed with black vertical borders.

By the final sampled frame, the scene has changed to an aircraft flying against a pale sky. The plane is shown in side view, angled slightly upward, with a red/orange “W” marking near the front and a stripe running along the fuselage. At the bottom of the frame, a red square logo with a white “W” appears next to the visible text: “The Western way to fly.”

No people are clearly visible in the sampled frames. The transition between the tropical shoreline image and the airplane/end-card is not shown, so any intervening action is unclear.
